_doc: Challenge deck + per-station bindings for the TTC 2026 map template.
Edit this file by hand to author challenges; re-run the seeder to push
changes into the database (no migration needed).
Shape:
- `deck`: metadata for the single `challenge_decks` row this file seeds.
- `templateName`: the `map_templates.name` whose nodes get bound.
(`map_templates` has no `code` column — name is the catalog key.)
- `stations`: keys are `map_template_nodes.code`; values are arrays of
challenges. Array index is `sort_order` on `map_template_node_challenges`,
so `stations.bay[0]` is the top-of-queue (and the only one the MVP
engine exposes today via `LIMIT 1 ORDER BY sort_order ASC`).
Challenge entry fields:
- `code` (required): unique within the deck. Stored on `challenges.code`.
- `type` (optional, default `task`): resolves against
`challenge_types.code` (currently `travel` / `photo` / `task`).
- `title` (required): short title shown in the modal header.
- `description` (required, empty string allowed): full prompt body.
- `flavorText` (optional, default null): atmospheric one-liner.
- `imageUrl` (optional, default null): absolute URL path. Files in
`client/public/challenges/<name>.<ext>` are served at
`/challenges/<name>.<ext>` in both dev and prod. External URLs are
accepted verbatim.
`bay` is filled in as a worked example you can copy from; the other 22
tile stations carry placeholder entries to be filled in over time.

deck.name: TTC 2026 — Standard
deck.description: Default challenge deck for the TTC 2026 map template.

bay.title: Bay Station
bay.description: Wow! You've just found $3000 on the ground. It's your lucky day! Find distinct items to spend the money on and catalog your hypothetical purchases. You cannot be under by more than $100. Tax is not included in the purchase price.

high-park.title: High Park Station
high-park.description:
Find the Maple Leaf at High Park and answer four trivia questions. You must get at least two correct to pass. One player must be the test taker, and the other must be the proctor. The proctor must quiz the test taker on the following four questions. The proctor may not collaborate with the test taker in any way once the questions have been revealed and may only confirm if the test taker has gotten the question correct or not without revealing the answer itself.

Q: What is the official sport of Canada? 
A: Lacrosse

Q: Which Canadian province is known as the "Land of 100 000 Lakes"?
A: Manitoba

Q: What bird appears on the Canadian one-dollar coin?
A: Loon

Q: Which Canadian province is the largest by area?
A: Québec

high-park.title: High Park Station
high-park.description:
Find the Maple Leaf at High Park and answer four trivia questions. You must get at least two correct to pass. One player must be the test taker, and the other must be the proctor. The proctor must quiz the test taker on the following four questions. The proctor may not collaborate with the test taker in any way once the questions have been revealed and may only confirm if the test taker has gotten the question correct or not without revealing the answer itself.

Q: What Canadian body of water experiences a lower force of gravity than the rest of the world? 
A: Hudson Bay

Q: What condiment is Quebéc the world's top producer of?
A: Maple syrup

Q: Which event was the most watched broadcast in Canadian history?
A: The 2010 Olympic Men's Hockey Final

Q: People in Churchill, Manitoba leave their cars unlocked for people escaping which animal?
A: Polar bears

high-park.title: High Park Station
high-park.description:
Find the Maple Leaf at High Park and answer four trivia questions. You must get at least two correct to pass. One player must be the test taker, and the other must be the proctor. The proctor must quiz the test taker on the following four questions. The proctor may not collaborate with the test taker in any way once the questions have been revealed and may only confirm if the test taker has gotten the question correct or not without revealing the answer itself.

Q: What is Canada's most played sport? 
A: Golf

Q: Project Atigi is the name of a collaboration that aims to combine the garments with traditional Inuit culture and designs between Inuit designers and which high-end parka brand?
A: Canada Goose

Q: In 1980, which athlete and Canadian hero, who was equipped with a prosthetic right leg, ran more than 5000 kilometres across the country to raise money for cancer research in his "Marathon of Hope"?
A: Terry Fox

Q: The name Canada is generally accepted as coming from the St. Lawrence Iroquoian word "kanata", meaning what?
A: Village/settlement

dufferin.title: Dufferin Station
dufferin.description: Route 29 is colloquially known as the "Sufferin' Dufferin'" bus route. Travel one stop on this route either on a bus or by foot.
dufferin.flavorText: 😩 Ride the Sufferin' Dufferin' Bus Route 🚌

spadina.title: Spadina Station
spadina.description: Spadina is a meeting point of lines 1 and 2. Roll a die: If it's 5 or higher, you can collect the tile at Spadina and skip the challenge at a station on line 1 or 2 that is the die's number away from Spadina (Spadina not included). Otherwise, you must wait five minutes before you can collect the tile from Spadina station and continue on your way.

dupont.title: Dupont Station
dupont.description: Build your own castle near Casa Loma! Build a self-supporting structure that is at least six inches tall and six inches wide.
dupont.flavorText: It's not the size that matters 😏

bathurst.title: Bathurst Station
bathurst.description: The "Korean Village Restaurant" is home to the NICEST MAN in Toronto! As remembrance of all the free food he' given us, visit his restaurant and leave a Google review. You do not need to enter the restaurant.

yorkdale.title: Yorkdale Station
yorkdale.description: Evelyn is hiding somewhere in Yorkdale mall, and you have to find her! Luckily for you, she was spotted in the image below. Find her location and take a selfie in the same spot.

yorkdale.description: Evelyn is hiding somewhere in Yorkdale mall, and you have to find her! Luckily for you, she was spotted in the image below. Find her location and take a selfie in the same spot.

museum.title: Museum Station
museum.description: There are a lot of old items at the ROM, but I want to see something truly unique! Find an ancient relic that is at least 50 years old and attempt to identify its age. The relice must not be a building, but it can be any other structure or item. The designated historian on your team gets two attempts at "dating" their chosen relic, and these two attempts may be used on the same relic or different relics. If the historian is wrong, they may not learn the true age of the relic (the other person must verify). The historian is allowed an error range of ten years.

queens-park.title: Queen's Park Station
queens-park.description: Time for a photo op! Take a photo with a bona fide queen. One player must take a selfie with a queen; this may be a portrait, a statue, or any visual representation of a queen.

st-andrew.title: St. Andrew Station
st-andrew.description: Much like everyday finance bros, we're going to partake in soe rudimentary analytics. Pick any establishment's front door and observe the passage of people through it. Afterward, guess how many people will pass through the doorway in the next five minutes. Following your guess, record and observe the amount of people passing through the doorway in that time. You complete the challenge if the number of people passing is within 15% of your guess. If the establishment has more than one front door, pick any one doorway. Every passage through the doorway is a unique instance (the same person can pass three times, and it will count as three people passing through).

union.title: Union Station
union.description: The CN Tower is 553 metres tall. Starting at the base of the CN tower, travel 553m in any direction. At the end of your journey, you must still be able to view the CN tower.

union.title: Union Station - Beaver
union.description: Take a selfie with Infinite Welcome beaver! They are somewhere in the Union Station great hall.

union.title: Union Station - Beaver
union.description: Take a selfie with Panama beaver! They are somewhere in the Union Station food court.

king.title: King Station
king.description: Treat yourself with a five-course meal! The meal must consist of five distinct food items: Appetizer, soup, entrée, salad, and dessert. Each item must loosely fit within the definition of a coures (i.e. you cannot have five different candies as all five courses, but water can be considered a "soup"). Only one player needs to complete this challenge.

king.title: King Station - Beaver
king.description: Take a selfie with Japan beaver! They are somewhere in the King Edward Hotel.

king.title: King Station - Beaver
king.description: Take a selfie with Canada beaver! They are somewhere in Berczy Park.

king.title: King Station - Beaver
king.description: Take a selfie with Netherlands beaver! They are somewhere in the King Edward Hotel.

queen.title: Queen Station
queen.description: Let out your inner fashionista! One player must describe a clothing item that they would wear as a queen. It must have three distinct and recognizable features that are agreed upon. Then, the other player must go into Eaton Center and find and take a picture of a piece of clothing that matches that description. Features must have a form and a colour attributed to them (ex. a red shirt with a white collar and black buttons).

queen.title: Queen Station - Beaver
queen.description: Take a selfie with Paraguay beaver! They are near the Canadian Venture Building.

college.title: College Station
college.description: One player is a visiting professor from the prestigious TEMU College, and they will share their knowledge. They must pick a category (however specific) of a topic that they are knowledgable in and teach a five minute course on it. The other player must then take an online quiz (ex. Sporcle, Quiz.com, etc.) on that subject and pass the course by scoring at least 50%. The subject cannot be mutually agreed upon and cannot be (to the best of your judgement) something that the other player is knowledgeable in (ex. "Indie games that Edward likes"). The quiz cannot be vetted beforehand, and the questions can only be viewed on attempt.

bloor-yonge.title: Bloor-Yonge Station
bloor-yonge.description: I'm thinking of a number...1000! Find a book at the nearest library. The designated reader of the group must read a short story of at least 1000 words out loud to their partner. Happy reading!

st-clair.title: St. Clair Station
st-clair.description: St. Clair Station's name came from the 1852 novel "Uncle Tom's Cabin", which has its namesake character of Augustine St. Clair. Find this novel in a nearby bookstore; if not available, find any piece of writing by the same author.

eglinton.title: Eglinton Station
eglinton.description: Time to play a playground classic: "Hot and Cold"! One player must secretly pick an establishment at least five minutes away by walking from Eglinton station. The other player, starting at Eglinton station, must ask questions about the location relative to other locations, and the first player must answer "hot" if the direction or location they have chosen is closer than their previous guess (ex. Q: "Is it the library?" A: "Hot"). The guesser is permitted up to ten questions. You may progress once the location has been correctly guessed.

aga-khan.title: Aga Khan Park & Museum Station
aga-khan.description: Photograph three different flowers in or around Aga Khan Park.

golden-mile.title: Golden Mile Station
golden-mile.description: Travel 1.6 miles (verifiable by Strava or any other distance tracker).

warden.title: Warden Station
warden.description: Photograph three distinct non-human animals around Warden station.

main-street.title: Main Street Station
main-street.description: Prove your athletic prowess! Go to East Toronto Athletic Field and play a game of "Red Light, Green Light". One player will stand at the destination and face away from the runner. While their back is turned, the runner must try to move to reach the first player, but must freeze when the other player turns around. The designated runner must start at a distance of 50 feet from their teammate and must win (tap their teammate's shoulder) to progress. If the field is closed, the game may be played anywhere that is judged to besafe.

greenwood.title: Greenwood Station
greenwood.description: Greenwood station is home to a railyard. I'm sad to see these trains not running, so let's create our own! Create a 300 metre train route with at least one turn. At least two people (not including yourselves) should travel this train route in its entirety.

broadview.title: Broadview Station
broadview.description: Construct a bridge of at minimum 10 inches in span. The bridge can be made from any material you can find. The bridge must be self-supporting for at least ten seconds.
